summarization.a: A schematic diagram illustrating the setup of an ALD system involving precursor and carrier gas inlets.
summarization.b: A diagram depicting the flow of precursor gases through reaction zones and effluent collection in an ALD tool.

b.question: What gases will be present in the effluent zones?
b.answer: b	In the effluent zones the pure used gases can be found (carrier gas and precursor gas), but also volatile reaction products.

b.question: How is the purge step achieved in this setup?
b.answer: b	With a gas shield the different precursor gases are shielded from eachother.

a.question: Does this setup allow for the adaption of supercycle ALD processes?
a.answer: a	For supercycle processes usually more than two reactants are required. This is not possible with this setup. The figure shows the inlet of just two precursor gases.

a.question: What is the vacuum pump used for?
a.answer: a	The pump is used to make sure that the sample is unable to move with respect to the sample table.
